category_title: Students with Intellectual Disability (ID) receiving individualized support in inclusive settings
student_description: This student requires a delicate balance between individualized academic support and social integration. They easily fall off-task without adapted curriculum and direct instructions, but become frustrated and socially isolated if given excessive 1-1 support when capable of independent work. They uniquely need a support system that provides targeted help to initiate tasks while actively promoting peer collaboration and sustained interaction with general education teachers.

criteria.rationale: This student needs adapted tasks and clear 1-1 directions to initiate engagement and prevent off-task behavior.
criteria.check: The tutor provides a specific, individualized instruction or adapts the task difficulty to help the student initiate the work.

criteria.rationale: Over-supporting this student when they are capable of independent work triggers frustration and social isolation.
criteria.check: The tutor steps back or offers the student space to work independently once the student demonstrates capability or expresses a desire to do so.

criteria.rationale: Having a specialized tutor exclusively manage behavior isolates the student from the mainstream classroom environment.
criteria.check: The tutor manages off-task behavior by redirecting the student's attention back to the mainstream class activity, peers, or general education teacher, rather than isolating them in a strict 1-1 dynamic.
